title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth blended jazz and pop at MGM Music Hall in Fenway during his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour.'
Puth's performance featured live improvisations and older hits, showcasing his deep connection to his musical craft.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey impressed with versatile performances, including live looping arrangements.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Jakob Dylan and The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly.
The concert featured a unique setlist, pairing Wallflowers songs with tracks from Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark'.
The encore included Wallflowers' hits and Petty classics 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting', delighting the sold-out crowd.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, marking the largest decline among Massachusetts cities.
Everett and Worcester recorded the biggest population gains with 2,362 and 1,916 new residents, respectively.
Massachusetts' population grew by 0.2% last year, significantly slower than the previous nearly 1% growth rate.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Demoulas Super Markets ranked on Forbes' list of largest U.S. family businesses.
Fidelity, controlled by the Johnson family, is ranked 14th; Demoulas is owned entirely by the Demoulas family and is 69th.
Forbes ranks businesses based on revenue and family ownership; Fidelity earns $37.7 billion, Demoulas $8 billion annually.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major campus housing overhaul to modernize and expand student amenities.
The project will be phased to allow current students to remain on-campus during renovations and construction.
Guided by housing studies, the initiative aims to maintain affordability and sustainability amid rising housing costs in Massachusetts.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed protest songs from her albums at the Chevalier Theatre in Medford on May 22.
Williams' setlist included covers of Bob Marley and Neil Young, and focused heavily on recent politically-themed songs.
Recovering from a 2020 stroke, Williams delivered a mix of old favorites and new material with social commentary.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle struck out nine over six innings, pulled after 85 pitches.
Reliever Justin Slaten gave up four hits and four runs in less than an inning against the Twins.
The Red Sox lost 8-6 to Minnesota, despite a strong offensive effort overshadowed by bullpen struggles.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens, 63, threw the first pitch at Fenway Park in a Red Sox City Connect jersey on Friday.
Clemens's son, Kody Clemens, a Minnesota Twins player, caught the pitch at his first Fenway appearance of the season.
The Red Sox distributed Roger Clemens bobbleheads to fans, commemorating his historical 20-strikeout games.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, securing his first NBA All-Defensive First Team honor in 2025-26.
White led NBA guards with 1.3 blocks per game and 552 contested shots during the 2025-26 season.
The Boston Celtics finished second in the Eastern Conference but exited the playoffs in the first round.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed' featuring Tatiana Maslany is streaming on Apple TV.
'Glory', an Oscar-winning Civil War drama, is available on Netflix until June 1.
Animated film 'Arco', featuring English voice cast including Mark Ruffalo and Natalie Portman, streams on Hulu.
